characters.name: Alex Taylor
characters.description: Alex TaylorAliasesAlexFamilyAngus Taylor (father)†Beth Taylor (mother)Adam Taylor (brother)Mike Taylor (uncle)Relationship(s)Ty DavisAffiliationNew York City Fire Department(FDNY)Physical descriptionStatusDeceasedGenderFemaleOccupationParamedicNationalityAmericanCharacter informationFirst appearanceJimmy's MountainLast appearanceThe Price of NobilityPortrayed byAmy Carlson Alexandra“Alex”Taylor worked in the New York City Fire Department (FDNY) as a Paramedic and Firefighter. She was portrayed byAmy Carlsonwho lasted from season two to season four. She had a strong bond with the guys and unfortunately got killed on an accident call. From a young age, Alex wanted to be a firefighter like her father, Angus. She played with toy fire trucks rather than with a Barbie. Her brother, Adam, became a nurse, leaving Alex free to follow the family tradition. Alex graduated from the fire academy and received from her father a Saint Florian medallion, one of her prized possessions.

characters.name: Bobby Caffey
characters.description: Roberto CaffeyAliasesBobbyFamilyMrs. Caffey (mother)Mr. Caffey (stepfather)Matteo "Matty" Caffey (brother)Renee Caffey (sister)Jenny Caffey (sister)Lisa Caffey (sister)Renee's son (nephew)Relationship(s)Kim Zambrano(best friend/Girlfriend)AffiliationNew York Fire Department(FDNY)Biographical informationMarital statusSingleDate of birth1972Place of birthNew York CityPhysical descriptionStatusDeceasedGenderMaleOccupationParamedicNationalityAmericanCharacter informationFirst appearanceWelcome to CamelotLast appearanceUnfinished BusinessPortrayed byBobby CannavaleSteven Strait(teenager) Roberto "Bobby" Caffeyworked for the New York City Fire Department (FDNY) as a Paramedic. He was portrayed by Bobby Cannavale and was on from season one to season two. Bobby was born in 1973 or 1974. His father left the family when he was a youngster and died many years later. The relationship did not really recover. Bobby was devoted to his family: his mother, three sisters and older brother Matteo (or "Matty"). Matty fell into using drugs, so despite being the younger of the 2 Caffey brothers, Bobby became the "man of the family". Bobby always thought it was his fault that his father left. His mother later remarried and the whole family adopted the last name "Caffey".

characters.name: Brendan Finney
characters.description: Brendan Finney Brendan Finney is a New York City Police Department (NYPD) police officer who worked at the 55th Precinct. He was portrayed by Josh Stewart in the final season of the show. Brendan is the son of Cathal "CT" Finney, who was a captain in IAB. Brendan deeply admired his father, although at times their relationship was not that close. Despite the rocky relationship Brendan had with his Father he still loved him. It was his father who first taught him to shoot when he was young. Brendan comes from a long line of cops, and regards it as "the family business." He's a bit rough around the edges and often has attitude problems. In his spare time he likes to box, and is also a good shooter.

characters.name: Brooke Carney
characters.description: Brooke CarneyAliasesBrooke DohertyFamilyJoey Doherty (ex-stepson)Relationship(s)Jimmy Doherty(ex-husband)AffiliationNew York Police DepartmentBiographical informationMarital statusDivorcedDate of birth1966Physical descriptionStatusAliveGenderFemaleOccupationOfficerNationalityAmericanCharacter informationFirst appearanceOfficer InvolvedLast appearanceHonorPortrayed byEva LaRue OfficerBrooke Carneyis a police officer from Mercy Hospital and the ex-wife ofJimmy Doherty. She was portrayed byEva LaRuefrom the beginning of the series to the 2nd season. She did not work at the 55 but was assigned to the 89 precincts. She met and fell in love with Jimmy Doherty at a supermarket, thanks to Jimmy's son Joey, and the pair got married. Brooke left Jimmy after finding out he'd slept with his friend Joe Lombardo's girlfriend at his engagement party and filed for divorce.

characters.name: Carlos Nieto
characters.description: Carlos NietoAliasesCarlFamilyUnnamed Father†Christian (brother)Kylie Samstron (daughter)Relationship(s)Holly Levine(wife)AffiliationNew York City Fire Department(FDNY)Biographical informationMarital statusMarriedDate of birthMay 11, 1975Place of birthHonolulu, HawaiiPhysical descriptionStatusAliveGenderMaleOccupationFirefighterNationalityFilipino-AmericanCharacter informationFirst appearanceWelcome to Camelot(1x01)Last appearanceGoodbye to Camelot(6x22)Portrayed byAnthony Ruivivar Carlos Nieto is a paramedic with the New York City Fire Department (FDNY) on “Third Watch”. He was portrayed byAnthony Ruivivarand remained on the show for all six seasons. In the Series finale he proposed marriage to Holly Levine and the pair got married. His Field Training Officer, partner and best friend was Monte “Doc” Parker. They worked together from the pilot episode until Doc left after he shot down a Captain who was known for shutting down firehouses. Carlos and Doc saw each other for the last time in the Series finale. Carlos Nieto was born on May 11, 1975, in Hawaii. Born of Filipino heritage, he was originally named Adam, and had an older brother named Christian. After their parents split up, Christian stayed with their mother, while their father took Adam to New York. Their father was the victim of a hit-and-run vehicle accident, which killed him. Young Carlos was found holding his father's hand. In the aftermath of his father's death, Carlos was placed in an orphanage run by nuns, where he grew up not knowing about his past and believing he had been abandoned by his mother at the orphanage. He went through 6 or 7 foster homes, and one of his foster mothers was named Lucinda Harding.

characters.name: Fred Yokas
characters.description: Fred YokasAliasesFredFamilyEmily Yokas(daughter)Charlie Yokas (son)Relationship(s)Faith Yokas(Ex-Wife)Biographical informationMarital statusDivorcedPhysical descriptionStatusAliveGenderMaleCharacter informationFirst appearanceWelcome to CamelotLast appearanceLeap of FaithPortrayed byChris Bauer Frederick "Fred" Yokaswas New York City Police Department (NYPD) OfficerFaith Yokas' ex-husband. He was portrayed by Chris Bauer who had a recurring role in the show's first two seasons and later became a series regular. Fred met Faith at college, where he was on the wrestling & football team. They fell in love and got married, and later had two children: daughter Emily and son Charlie. However, the Yokas family soon hit financial difficulties, partly caused by Fred's growing alcohol problems. Faith's father had been an alcoholic and she did not want her children to end up with a father like hers.

characters.name: Jimmy Doherty
characters.description: Jimmy DohertyAliasesJimmyFamilyTommy Doherty (brother)Jack Doherty (brother)Joseph "Joey" James Doherty (son)Kevin James Doherty (son)Relationship(s)Kim Zambrano(wife)Brooke Doherty (ex-wife)AffiliationNew York City Fire Department(FDNY)RankLieutenantBiographical informationMarital statusMarriedPlace of birthNew York CityPhysical descriptionStatusAliveGenderMaleOccupationFirefighterNationalityAmericanCharacter informationFirst appearanceWelcome to CamelotLast appearanceGoodbye to CamelotPortrayed byEddie Cibrian James”Jimmy”Doherty worked for the New York City Fire Department (FDNY) as a Firefighter. He was portrayed byEddie Cibrianfrom 1999 to 2004. He made an appearance in the show's final season. Jimmy [born in 1968] originally wanted to become a baseball player, and even tried out for the New York Yankees in 1993. He failed to make the team and joined the New York City Fire Department (FDNY) as his "back-up" option. This soon became his chosen profession.

characters.name: John Sullivan
characters.description: John SullivanAliasesSullyFamilyIrene Sullivan (mother)Sergei† (stepson)Fran (sister)Mike (ex-brother-in-law)RelativesTatiana† (wife)AffiliationNew York City Police Department(NYPD)Biographical informationMarital statusWidowedPlace of birthNew YorkPhysical descriptionStatusAliveGenderMaleOccupationPolice officerCharacter informationFirst appearanceWelcome to Camelot(1x01)Last appearanceGoodbye to Camelot(6x22)Portrayed bySkipp Sudduth John "Sully" Sullivan was a New York City Police Department (NYPD) police officer who worked at the 55th Precinct. He was portrayed bySkipp Sudduthfor all 6 seasons. In the series finale he decided he wanted to retire. John Sullivan was born in New York in 1957. He has a sister, Fran, who later moved to Orlando, Florida with her now ex-husband Mike. Sully joined the New York City Police Department (NYPD), starting work at the 55th precinct where his training officer was Tyrone Davis, Jr. The two men eventually became partners and were like brothers. However the two soon realized that there was a group of corrupt cops, including Cathal Finney, who discovered that Ty Sr was planning on turning the others in. He arranged for Ty Sr to be murdered in order to silence him. This hit Sully a lot, he stopped going to church after this day and described as "the worst day of his life". Sully later found out the truth about the murder but said nothing in order to protect the Davis family and to make sure Maggie Davis still got her late husband's pension.

characters.name: Kim Zambrano
characters.description: Kimberly ZambranoAliasesKimFamilyCatherine Zambrano (mother)Melanie Zambrano (sister)Joey Doherty (son)Kevin Doherty (son)Relationship(s)Jimmy Doherty(husband/ex-husband)AffiliationNew York City Fire Department(FDNY)Biographical informationMarital statusMarriedPhysical descriptionStatusAliveGenderFemaleOccupationParamedicNationalityAmericanCharacter informationFirst appearanceWelcome to CamelotLast appearanceGoodbye to CamelotPortrayed byKim Raver Kimberly"Kim"Zambrano was a paramedic with the New York City Fire Department (FDNY). She was portrayed byKim Raverfrom the beginning of the show to the first episode of the last season. Her last “Real” appearance was in the series finale. Kim was one of two children and grew up with her single mother. Her mother married several times but also continually flew around the country in search of her husband so that Kim and her sister Melanie could grow up with a father.

characters.name: Lieutenant Bob Swersky
characters.description: Bob Swersky was a Lieutenant in the New York City Police Department (NYPD). He was in charge of the 55th precinct. He was portrayed by Joe Lisi for the whole series. Biography Fair but firm, he was looked upon as a father figure by many of the cops, especially Bosco. He would often come to the aid of his officers when they were in trouble. After the series ended, he retired in order to spend time with his grandchildren.

characters.name: Maritza Cruz
characters.description: Sergeant Maritza Cruz Maritza Cruz was a Sergeant in the New York City Police Department (NYPD) and was assigned to the 55th Precinct. She was portrayed by Tia Texada from season 4 to the end of the series. Cruz was an Anti-Crime Sergeant with the fictional 55th Precinct of the New York City Police Department (NYPD). She was often a renegade officer who does whatever it takes to get the job done. This is seen throughout her part in the series. This is proven during her commitment to duty which led her to being raped by a man while undercover.

characters.name: Monte Parker
characters.description: Monte "Doc" ParkerAliasesDocFamilyJohn Parker (father)Mrs. Parker† (mother)4 brothersRelationship(s)Debra Parker† (wife)Dr.Sarah MoralesAffiliationNew York Fire Department(FDNY)Biographical informationMarital statusDatingPlace of birthNew York CityPhysical descriptionStatusAliveGenderMaleOccupationNew York City Fire Department (FDNY) ParamedicNationalityAmericanCharacter informationFirst appearanceWelcome to CamelotLast appearanceGoodbye to CamelotPortrayed byMichael Beach Monte"Doc"Parker was a paramedic in the New York City Fire Department (FDNY). He was portrayed byMichael Beachfrom the beginning of the series to the 5th season. He made a guest appearance during the last season. Parker was born in 1962 in New York City, one of 5 sons of John and Mrs. Parker. As a youngster he accidentally killed his best friend over a girl, something that would come back and haunt him in the future. After high school, Monte spent four years at City College, doing a course in business and administration. After graduating, he spent 2 years selling stereos before switching to office suppliers. Bored, Monte considered joining the Air Force, but was turned down due to poor eyesight. He ended up joining the New York City Fire Department (FDNY) in 1985 as a paramedic, after seeing paramedics in the street and thinking the job looked good. He acquired the nickname "Doc" and was partnered up with Jerry Markowitz, the two regarded themselves as brothers. Jerry helped Doc overcome the death of his wife Debra, who was killed in a boating accident in 1998. Doc and Debra's last words to each other had been an argument.

characters.name: Paramedic Holly Levine
characters.description: Holly Levine is a paramedic of the New York City Fire Department (FDNY). She was portrayed by Yvonne Jung and lasted on the show from season five to the end of the show. In the Series Finale of Third Watch She and Carlos Nieto got Married. Biography Not much information is given on Holly, but she dated paramedic Carlos Nieto. In the series finale, Carlos proposed to Holly and they got married. They later moved to Staten Island where they had three children.

characters.description: Sasha Monroe Sasha Monroe was a New York City Police Department (NYPD) police officer who worked at the 55th Precinct. She was portrayed by Nia Long and lasted from season five to the end of the series. Biography

characters.name: Ty Davis
characters.description: Tyrone "Ty" Davis Jr.AliasesTyFamilyTyrone Davis Sr.† (father)Maggie Davis (mother)Renata Davis (sister)Gina Moore [nee Davis] (sister)Mr. Moore (brother-in-law)Gwen Girard (half-sister)Jeanne (grandmother)Relationship(s)Sasha Monroe(fiancée)AffiliationNew York City Police Department(NYPD)Biographical informationMarital statusFiancéPhysical descriptionStatusAliveGenderMaleOccupationPolice officerCharacter informationFirst appearanceWelcome to CamelotLast appearanceGoodbye to CamelotPortrayed byCoby Bell Tyrone "Ty" Davis Jr.is a New York City Police Department (NYPD) police officer who worked at the 55th precinct. He was portrayed by Coby Bell and stayed on the show for all 6 seasons. Ty was born the only son of his parents, Tyrone Sr. and Maggie Davis. He has 2 sisters. When Ty was 12, his father, also a New York City Police Department (NYPD) cop, was killed in the line of duty. Ty Sr. was a corrupt cop, something Ty Jr. was unaware of until later in life. He was involved in a group of corrupt cops but decided to report them to Internal Affairs Bureau (IAB). Another member of the group, Cathal Finney, arranged for Ty Sr. to be murdered by a criminal named Raymond Morris and make it look like an accident. Ty Jr’s partner, John 'Sully' Sullivan, found out the truth, but decided not to report it so that Maggie would still be entitled to her late husband's pension.
